title: Create subitems from multi select when item is created
excerpt: Is there a way to automatically create subitems from the selections made on the multi-select question? Currently, when an item is created, that item goes to a board that was selected in the single select question on a fo…

posts.author: jgrabin (Joel Grabin)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to automatically create subitems from the selections made on the multi-select question? Currently, when an item is created, that item goes to a board that was selected in the single select question on a form. What I am hoping to also happen, is to automatically create sub-items for that item. These sub-items are chosen in the multi-select question on the same form as the single select question. Can it be done or is there a better way to do it.

posts.author: omid_at_rally (Omid Ebrahimi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Joel!

Your interest in automating the creation of sub-items based on selections made in a multi-select question from a form is understandable. This would definitely streamline your workflow.

Monday.com does not have built-in functionality for automatically creating sub-items from multi-select form answers. While you can map form responses to various item columns, automatically generating sub-items from a multi-select question is not a feature offered directly within the platform.

However, our team of developers specializes in creating custom enhancements and integrations for monday.com. We might be able to help develop a tailored solution that would enable you to automate the creation of sub-items based on the selections made in the multi-select question.

You can reach me at omid@rallyessentials.com

posts.author: YashGarg (Yash Garg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jgrabin,

We recently launched the app Special Automations - monday.com App Marketplace which has the required automation recipe for your use case.

posts.cookedHtml: Is creating subitems from a form able to be done at all? Such as from a single select question?
